rationale: The citing paper references van Heerde, Leeflang, and Wittink (2000) in both the introduction and literature review as direct evidence that promotion effects include pre/post-promotion dynamics and some primary demand expansion. That evidence is used to justify the paper's core motivation: moving beyond brand-switching accounts toward a model of stockpiling and flexible consumption in competitive pricing. Although the citing paper does not adopt the cited paper's estimation method, it relies on the cited findings to frame why its theory and hypotheses are needed.
relation_description: The citing paper treats van Heerde et al. (2000) as part of the empirical basis showing that promotion effects are dynamic and can include primary demand expansion. This prior evidence helps ground the citing paper's conceptual setup and motivates its game-theoretic analysis of price competition under stockpiling and flexible consumption.
